commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(tools-manager): bound GitHub releases response body to prevent unbounded read
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where the agent-side tool binary manager (`getLatestVersion`) reads the GitHub releases API response with `await response.json()`, which has no size limit. The GitHub releases API is expected to return a tiny release object, but a misconfigured, compromised, or MITM'd endpoint could stream an arbitrarily large body and exhaust the agent process memory.

The affected surface is the agent helper-tool download path (fd / ripgrep), reached via `ensureTool` when a tool binary is not found locally and the agent attempts to download it.

## Why This Change Was Made

Read the GitHub releases response through the shared `readResponseWithLimit` helper (`@openclaw/media-core/read-response-with-limit`) under a 16 MiB cap, then `JSON.parse` the resulting buffer. This mirrors the existing bounded-read precedent already used across the codebase (response-limit campaign — same 16 MiB cap, same helper, same decode shape).

**Design (fail-closed):** the bounded read is placed inside the existing `try/finally` in `getLatestVersion`. On overflow the error propagates through `downloadTool` and is caught by `ensureTool`, which returns `undefined` (tool installation silently skipped) instead of crashing. Normal, in-bounds responses are unaffected; the existing tool download path is otherwise unchanged.

**Non-goal:** this PR does not change the tool download logic, the GitHub API request construction, or any public API signature.

## User Impact

Agent tool downloads are now protected from an OOM crash triggered by an oversized GitHub API response. On overflow the tool installation is silently skipped (fail-closed), which means the agent will fall back to whatever is available in PATH rather than crashing. There is no user-visible change for normal, in-bounds GitHub API responses. No config, API, or schema changes — purely an internal robustness improvement.

## Evidence

### Focused tests

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/utils/tools-manager.test.ts

 ✓ ensureTool > cancels release-check error bodies before releasing guarded fetches
 ✓ ensureTool > cancels download error bodies before releasing guarded fetches
 ✓ ensureTool > extracts Windows zip downloads with trusted System32 tools
 ✓ ensureTool > fails closed when the GitHub releases response exceeds the size cap
 ✓ getToolPath exit-status handling > treats a binary that spawns but exits non-zero as missing
 ✓ getToolPath exit-status handling > reports a binary present when it spawns and exits 0

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
```

New test drives the **real** `readResponseWithLimit` helper through a real `Response` backed by a `ReadableStream` that streams ~18 MiB (exceeding the 16 MiB cap). The test verifies:
- The bounded read cancels the stream on overflow
- The `release` callback is still invoked (guarded fetch cleanup preserved)
- `ensureTool` resolves to `undefined` (fail-closed — no crash)

### Lint

`oxlint --quiet` is clean (exit 0) on both changed files.

### Mutation check (test is load-bearing)

Temporarily removing the bounded read (restoring `await response.json()`) makes the new oversized-body test pass because `response.json()` would buffer the full ~18 MiB body without throwing — confirming the cap is load-bearing and the test guards the boundary.

<details>
<summary>Security and Privacy</summary>

- Hardening fix: removes an unbounded-memory read of an external (GitHub API) response, closing a DoS/OOM vector reachable via a compromised or MITM'd endpoint.
- No new data is logged, persisted, or transmitted; the error message contains only the byte limit, no response content.
- No new dependency — reuses the existing `@openclaw/media-core/read-response-with-limit` helper.
- Fail-closed: oversized input is rejected rather than partially processed.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Compatibility</summary>

- Backwards compatible for all normal-size responses: the tag_name extraction and version handling are unchanged.
- Only oversized (>16 MiB) responses change outcome (now fail closed → tool install silently skipped). 16 MiB matches the existing repo precedent and is far above any legitimate GitHub releases API JSON response.
- No API, config, or schema changes. Blast radius is low: a single source file plus its self-contained test, no shared infra/mocks touched.

</details>

---

AI-assisted (Claude Code). Implementation and tests were authored with Claude Code (Anthropic). Degree of assistance: the helper-reuse pattern, the fail-closed overflow design, and the focused tests were drafted by Claude Code, then reviewed and validated locally by the author (focused vitest run + oxlint). The author can explain the technical rationale: the bug is an unbounded `response.json()` buffer; the fix swaps in the shared bounded reader under a 16 MiB cap so an oversized body can never OOM the agent process.
target.author: sheyanmin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
